title: IDOR — order invoices readable across tenants (/api/orders/{id}/invoice)
evidence: Test tenant B (uid=1042) fetched the invoice PDF of tenant A (order 5581) — HTTP 200, body is another tenant's document. No ownership check on the object id.

title: SSRF — image proxy fetches attacker-controlled URL (/api/proxy?url=)
evidence: Out-of-band callback received at oob.lab.example from the api.lab.example egress within 1.2s. Cloud metadata (169.254.169.254) returned 403 (blocked), so impact is currently OOB/blind — reported_by_tool, not escalated.

title: Permissive CORS with credentials (Origin reflected + ACAC:true) on /api/account
evidence: Access-Control-Allow-Origin reflects an arbitrary Origin AND Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true on /api/account — a malicious page can read the logged-in tenant's profile/session data cross-origin.

title: Password reset tokens predictable (sequential counter, no expiry, no rate limit)
evidence: Reset tokens are zero-padded counters correlated with request time; a victim's token was guessed within ~300 attempts with no rate limiting and no token expiry. Full account takeover of the storefront tenant.
poc: N/A — multi-step; see write-up REFERENCE_ENGAGEMENT.md section 4

title: Origin server IP exposed behind CDN (passive DNS history + shared TLS SAN)
evidence: Historical A record plus a shared certificate SAN reveal origin 203.0.113.24 (RFC 5737 doc range), letting an attacker bypass the CDN WAF and hit the origin directly. Info-disclosure only on its own.
poc: N/A — passive DNS/certificate correlation (no traffic to target)

title: Missing security headers + outdated jQuery flagged by template scan
evidence: No CSP and no HSTS on shop.lab.example; jQuery 1.12.4 (known DOM-XSS sinks) is served. Hardening gap surfaced by the vulnerability-scan template pass.
